content_en:
Abu Qatadah, Al-Harith Ibn Rib*yy Al-Ansaaryy, testified that the prisoners offered Salaah, while others said they neither gave Adhaan nor offered prayer. The prisoners retired for the night. At the dead of night, Khalid ordered that the night being cold, the prisoners should be kept warm, but this order was misunderstood to be an order for murder. Accordingly, Malik and his male companions were killed. It was Daraar Ibn

Al-Azwad who murdered Malik [bn Nuwayrah. Having seen the Malik and his companions killed, Khalid remarked, "If Allah wills that something should occur, it surely occurs.”

After the death of Malik, the entire tribe of Bani Tameem surrendered and professed faith in Islam. Khalid immediately married Layla, the beautiful widow of Malik Ibn Nuwayrah.

The campaign against the Bani Tameem was a masterstroke from the political point of view. It brought the entire tribe to the fold of Islam. From the military point of view the significance of the action at Bitaah was that the rear of Sajaah. the false Prophetess, was cut off, and she could no longer count on the support of Bani Tameem.

The episode, however, led to considerable scandal. In some quarters it was held that Malik was indeed a Muslim, and that he had been murdered merely because Khalid coveted his pretty wife. Some of the Ansaar in the army of Khalid led by Abu Qatadah refused to fight under the command of Khalid. Abu Qatadah along with Malik's brother set out for Madeenah to lodge a complaint against Khalid before the Caliph. MuTameem was a distinguished poet, and he composed an elegy mourning the death of his brother, and condemning Khalid as his murderer. These verses became popular in Madeenah and those who listened to them grieved at the murder of Malik.

Khalid was summoned to Madeenah and was asked to clarify his position. There were two charges against him: murdering a Muslim man and marrying his wife. Khalid's defense of himself was that if according to the Prophet, peace

and blessings be upon him, he was the "Sword of Allah", such sword could not fall on the neck of a Muslim.

On entering the Grand Mosque in his rusty armor with arrows stuck in his turban, Khalid was noticed by ~ Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, who went up to him, pulled out the arrows and broke them into pieces. He said threateningly: "You have killed a Muslim in cold blood and taken his wife for yours! By Allah! You deserve to be stoned!" Nevertheless, when Khalid met the Caliph, he managed to defend himself and was excused, though he did not escape being criticized for his disgraceful marriage with Layla.

“Umar was of the view that Khalid was to be blamed, and he should be suitably punished. Abu Bakr felt that a military commander, Khalid was indispensable. His view was that even if it was held that Khalid was guilty of a lapse, such lapse could be passed over in the broader interest of Islam. Musaylamah in the Yamamah valley was posing a great threat to the Muslims. Two Muslim Generals sent against Musaylamah had suffered defeat. The position was critical, and at that stage a General of the caliber of Khalid alone could vindicate the honor of Islam. Abu Bakr decided to overlook the lapse of Khalid, and directed him to undertake operations against Musaylamah. As_ there were doubts whether Malik was or was not a Muslim, Abu Bakr decided that blood money should be paid out of the Baytul-Maal to the heirs of Malik for his murder.

“Umar did not feel happy at this decision of Abu Bakr. When ~Umar remonstrated, Abu Bakr observed: "Umar, I cannot sheathe the sword, which Allah has intended to be wielded against the non-Muslims."

Campaign Against Musaylamah Al-Khadhaab (The Liar):

Of all the imposters and false Prophet s who rose in Arabia after the death of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, the most notorious and dangerous was Musaylamah Al-Khadhaab (the liar), who belonged to the Banu Haneefah tribe of Central Arabia.

Musaylamah visited Madeenah during the lifetime of the Prophet, and enjoyed the privilege of his company for some time. On return from Madeenah, Musaylamah, however, claimed himself to be a Prophet of Allah. He laid claim to a divine mission and formed a new creed, where he absolved his followers from the obligations of Siyaam and Zakaah, reduced the number of daily prayers, and made adultery and drinking lawful to the people. He also forbade his followers to cohabit with their wives once they had become mothers. In imitation of the Glorious Quran he recited rhythmical sentences and bits of doggerel, which he had himself composed, but gave out as having been revealed by Almighty Allah.

Musaylamah had an attractive personality and was endowed with a superb physique. He was a good speaker, and could sway the masses. He exploited these qualities, and succeeded in winning over a considerable following.

With his illness becoming serious, the Prophet , peace and blessings be upon him, asked Musaylamah to abandon his pretensions. However, Musaylamah sent an impudent letter to the Prophet, saying: "From Musylamah, the Prophet of Allah, to Muhammad, the Prophet of Allah. Surely Allah has made me your partner in Prophet hood. Let us divide the earth between ourselves." To this the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, replied: "In the Name of Allah, Most Gracious, Most Merciful. From Muhammad, the Prophet of Allah, to

Musaylamah the Liar! Peace be upon those who follow the truth. Surely the earth belongs to Allah, and gives it asa heritage to such of His servants as He wishes; and the sequel is for the righteous.

The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, deputed Nahr Ar-Rajjaal Ibn ~Unfuwah, a Muslim convert from the tribe of Banu Haneefah, to go back to his people in order to propagate Islam and refute the claims of Musaylamah. On return to his tribe, Nahr Ar-Rajjaal fell a victim to the blandishments of Musaylamah, and falsely declared that he was witness to the fact that the Prophet had acknowledged Musaylamah as co-sharer in the divine mission. That established the credentials of Musaylamah, and the number of his followers increased considerably.

With the death of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, Musaylamah gained in strength further. The general argument that prevailed with the people was that Muhammad, peace be upon him, was dead while Musaylamah was alive, and that a living Prophet was to be preferred to a dead one. Many tribes who were hostile to Islam joined his ranks.

Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, originally entrusted the operations to crush Musaylamah to “Tkrimah Ibn Abi Jahl, Sharhabee! Ibn Hasanah was to advance with another column to the assistance of ~Ikrimah. Though strict orders were given to “Ikrimah by Abu Bakr not to engage in a battle with the impostor till reinforcements arrived, he was so anxious to get the better of him that he immediately started fighting Musaylamah, and the Muslims were beaten back with considerable losses. When the news of the defeat reached Madeenah, Abu Bakr felt very much sad and distressed. Some time later, Sharhabeel arrived in the Yamaamah valley with his

column. He also launched an attack against Musaylamah without waiting for help, and was also defeated.

Two successive defeats created an embarrassing situation for the Muslims. That raised the morale of the followers of Musaylamah who proudly declared that Musaylamah was indeed a Prophet and divine help was on their side.

It was at this critical juncture that Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, commissioned Khalid Ibn al-Waleed, may Allah be pleased with him, to set off on a campaign against Musaylamah. Elaborate arrangements were made to reinforce the army of Khalid. Seasoned soldiers were included in the force under his command. Al-Baraa' Ibn Malik and Thabit Ibn Qays led the contingents of the Ansaar, while Abu Hadhayfah and Zayd led the contingent of the Muhajreen. Against his declared policy, Abu Bakr permitted the veterans of Badr to join the forces of Khalid. Among others, those who joined the force were “Abdur Rahmaan Ibn Abi Bakr, * Abdullaah Ibn ~Umar, and Abu Dujaanah the renowned warrior of Uhud, may Allah be pleased with them all.

The Battle of * Aqraba:

accompanied him to the battlefield. Khalid ordered him to care of her.

Musaylamah intercepted the advance of the Muslim army at the plain of ~ Aqraba. Here the two opposing armies arranged their ranks for the encounter. The battle that followed was hotly contested. The forces of Musaylamah numbered over 4,000, while the strength of the Muslim army did not exceed 13,000. Besides being outnumbered the Muslim forces suffered from certain disabilities. There were differences between the tribes, the Ansaar and the Muhajreen.

When the battle of *Agraba flared up there was Musaylamah's eloquent son, Sharhabeel, to egg the troops on and boost their morale. He reminded them of Khalid 's cruelty and warned them that unless they fought bravely their women would be taken captives.

A dust storm blew across the valley against the faces of the Muslims. Taking advantage of this, the forces of Musaylamah increased their pressure, and the Muslims had to fall back. Some men of the Banu Haneefah even reached the tent of Khalid where Majaa*ah was in chains guarded by Layla. These men wanted to kill Layla, and rescue Majaa*ah. Mujaa*ah called upon them to desist from raising their hands against a woman. He wanted them to go and kill some men. These men left the camp, and said that they would return after some time to rescue Majaa*ah. In the confusion that followed, the party could not come back, and in the meantime the Muslims were able to take precautionary measures.

When the battle for the first day ended, there was Jubilation in the camp of Musaylamah. Though Khalid had been forced to withdraw he refused to admit defeat. He regrouped his army in tribal commands and exhorted the various tribes to

show their valor on the battlefield. From within the Makkah and Madinite horsemen he created a reserve force of a thousand cavalrymen and kept them under his personal command.
content_id:
Abu Qatadah, Al-Harith Ibn Rib'yy Al-Ansaaryy, bersaksi bahwa para tawanan melaksanakan salah, sementara yang lain mengatakan mereka tidak mengumandangkan adzan maupun mendirikan salah. Para tawanan beristirahat untuk malam itu. Di tengah malam, Khalid memerintahkan agar para tawanan dijaga kehangatannya karena malam yang dingin, namun perintah ini disalahartikan sebagai perintah pembunuhan. Akibatnya, Malik dan para sahabat laki-lakinya dibunuh. Dialah Daraar Ibn

Al-Azwad yang membunuh Malik Ibn Nuwayrah. Setelah menyaksikan Malik dan para sahabatnya terbunuh, Khalid berkata, "Jika Allah menghendaki sesuatu terjadi, maka hal itu pasti terjadi."

Setelah kematian Malik, seluruh suku Bani Tameem menyerah dan mengucapkan keimanan kepada Islam. Khalid segera menikahi Layla, janda cantik Malik Ibn Nuwayrah.

Kampanye melawan Bani Tameem merupakan langkah cemerlang dari sudut pandang politik. Kampanye ini membawa seluruh suku ke dalam pangkuan Islam. Dari sudut pandang militer, signifikansi tindakan di Bitaah adalah bahwa barisan belakang Sajaah, nabi palsu perempuan itu, telah terputus, dan ia tidak lagi dapat mengandalkan dukungan Bani Tameem.

Namun, peristiwa ini menimbulkan skandal yang cukup besar. Di beberapa kalangan, diyakini bahwa Malik memang seorang Muslim, dan bahwa ia dibunuh semata-mata karena Khalid menginginkan istrinya yang cantik. Sebagian kaum Anshar dalam pasukan Khalid yang dipimpin oleh Abu Qatadah menolak berperang di bawah komando Khalid. Abu Qatadah bersama saudara Malik berangkat ke Madeenah untuk mengajukan pengaduan terhadap Khalid kepada Khalifah. Mu'tameem adalah seorang penyair terkemuka, dan ia menggubah sebuah elegi meratapi kematian saudaranya, serta mengecam Khalid sebagai pembunuhnya. Syair-syair ini menjadi populer di Madeenah dan mereka yang mendengarnya berduka atas pembunuhan Malik.

Khalid dipanggil ke Madeenah dan diminta untuk menjelaskan posisinya. Ada dua tuduhan terhadapnya: membunuh seorang Muslim dan menikahi istrinya. Pembelaan diri Khalid adalah bahwa jika menurut Nabi, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, ia adalah "Pedang Allah", maka pedang semacam itu tidak mungkin jatuh ke leher seorang Muslim.

Saat memasuki Masjid Agung dengan baju besinya yang berkarat dan anak-anak panah menancap di surbannya, Khalid diperhatikan oleh Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, yang mendekatinya, mencabut anak-anak panah itu dan mematahkannya. Ia berkata dengan penuh ancaman: "Engkau telah membunuh seorang Muslim dengan darah dingin dan mengambil istrinya untuk dirimu sendiri! Demi Allah! Engkau layak dirajam!" Namun demikian, ketika Khalid menemui Khalifah, ia berhasil membela dirinya dan dimaafkan, meski ia tidak luput dari kritikan atas pernikahannya yang memalukan dengan Layla.

Umar berpendapat bahwa Khalid patut disalahkan dan harus mendapat hukuman yang setimpal. Abu Bakr merasa bahwa sebagai seorang komandan militer, Khalid tidak tergantikan. Pendapatnya adalah bahwa sekalipun Khalid dianggap bersalah atas suatu kelalaian, kelalaian tersebut dapat diabaikan demi kepentingan Islam yang lebih luas. Musaylamah di lembah Yamamah sedang menimbulkan ancaman besar bagi kaum Muslimin. Dua Jenderal Muslim yang dikirim untuk menghadapi Musaylamah telah menderita kekalahan. Situasinya sangat genting, dan pada saat itu hanya seorang Jenderal sekaliber Khalid yang mampu menegakkan kehormatan Islam. Abu Bakr memutuskan untuk mengabaikan kelalaian Khalid, dan memerintahkannya untuk melancarkan operasi melawan Musaylamah. Karena terdapat keraguan apakah Malik seorang Muslim atau bukan, Abu Bakr memutuskan bahwa uang darah harus dibayarkan dari Baytul-Maal kepada ahli waris Malik atas pembunuhannya.

Umar tidak merasa puas dengan keputusan Abu Bakr ini. Ketika Umar mengajukan protes, Abu Bakr berkata: "Umar, aku tidak dapat menyarungkan pedang yang telah Allah kehendaki untuk dihunus melawan orang-orang kafir."

Kampanye Melawan Musaylamah Al-Khadhaab (Si Pendusta):

Di antara semua penipu dan nabi palsu yang muncul di Jazirah Arab setelah wafatnya Nabi, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, yang paling terkenal dan berbahaya adalah Musaylamah Al-Khadhaab (si pendusta), yang berasal dari suku Banu Haneefah di Arabia bagian tengah.

Musaylamah mengunjungi Madeenah semasa hidup Nabi, dan mendapat kesempatan menemani beliau selama beberapa waktu. Setelah kembali dari Madeenah, Musaylamah mengklaim dirinya sebagai nabi Allah. Ia mengaku memiliki misi ilahi dan membentuk keyakinan baru, di mana ia membebaskan pengikutnya dari kewajiban siyaam dan zakah, mengurangi jumlah salah harian, serta menghalalkan perzinaan dan minuman keras bagi masyarakat. Ia juga melarang pengikutnya untuk menggauli istri mereka setelah istri-istri tersebut menjadi ibu. Meniru Al-Quran Al-Karim, ia melantunkan kalimat-kalimat berirama dan syair-syair murahan yang ia karang sendiri, namun mengklaimnya sebagai wahyu dari Allah Yang Maha Agung.

Musaylamah memiliki kepribadian yang menarik dan dikaruniai fisik yang luar biasa. Ia seorang pembicara yang handal dan mampu mempengaruhi massa. Ia mengeksploitasi kualitas-kualitas ini dan berhasil mendapatkan pengikut yang cukup banyak.

Ketika sakitnya semakin parah, Nabi, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, meminta Musaylamah untuk meninggalkan klaim palsunya. Namun, Musaylamah mengirim surat yang kurang ajar kepada Nabi, yang berbunyi: "Dari Musaylamah, Nabi Allah, kepada Muhammad, Nabi Allah. Sesungguhnya Allah telah menjadikanku sebagai mitramu dalam kenabian. Marilah kita bagi bumi ini di antara kita berdua." Atas hal ini, Nabi, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, menjawab: "Bismillahirrahmanirrahim. Dari Muhammad, Nabi Allah, kepada

Musaylamah Si Pendusta! Keselamatan bagi mereka yang mengikuti kebenaran. Sesungguhnya bumi ini milik Allah, dan Dia wariskan kepada siapa saja di antara hamba-Nya yang Dia kehendaki; dan akibat yang baik adalah bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa."

Nabi, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, mengutus Nahr Ar-Rajjaal Ibn 'Unfuwah, seorang mualaf dari suku Banu Haneefah, untuk kembali kepada kaumnya guna menyebarkan Islam dan membantah klaim Musaylamah. Sesampainya di sukunya, Nahr Ar-Rajjaal terjerumus oleh bujuk rayu Musaylamah, dan dengan dusta menyatakan bahwa ia menjadi saksi bahwa Nabi telah mengakui Musaylamah sebagai rekan dalam misi ilahi. Hal itu memperkuat kredensial Musaylamah, dan jumlah pengikutnya pun bertambah secara signifikan.

Dengan wafatnya Nabi, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, Musaylamah semakin kuat. Argumen umum yang berlaku di kalangan masyarakat adalah bahwa Muhammad, shalallahu 'alaihi wasallam, telah wafat sementara Musaylamah masih hidup, dan bahwa nabi yang hidup lebih baik daripada nabi yang telah wafat. Banyak suku yang bermusuhan dengan Islam bergabung dalam barisannya.

Abu Bakr, radhiyallahu 'anhu, pada mulanya mempercayakan operasi penumpasan Musaylamah kepada 'Ikrimah Ibn Abi Jahl, sementara Sharhabeel Ibn Hasanah diperintahkan untuk bergerak maju dengan pasukan lain untuk membantu 'Ikrimah. Meski Abu Bakr telah memberikan perintah tegas kepada 'Ikrimah agar tidak terlibat dalam pertempuran dengan si penipu itu sampai bala bantuan tiba, ia sangat ingin mengalahkannya sehingga segera memulai pertempuran melawan Musaylamah, dan kaum Muslimin dipukul mundur dengan kerugian yang cukup besar. Ketika berita kekalahan itu sampai ke Madeenah, Abu Bakr merasa sangat sedih dan prihatin. Beberapa waktu kemudian, Sharhabeel tiba di lembah Yamaamah bersama

pasukannya. Ia pun melancarkan serangan terhadap Musaylamah tanpa menunggu bantuan, dan juga mengalami kekalahan.

Dua kekalahan berturut-turut menciptakan situasi yang memalukan bagi kaum Muslimin. Hal itu meningkatkan semangat para pengikut Musaylamah yang dengan bangga menyatakan bahwa Musaylamah memang seorang Nabi dan pertolongan ilahi berpihak kepada mereka.

Pada saat yang genting inilah Abu Bakr, radhiyallahu 'anhu, menugaskan Khalid Ibn al-Waleed, radhiyallahu 'anhu, untuk berangkat dalam kampanye melawan Musaylamah. Persiapan yang matang dilakukan untuk memperkuat pasukan Khalid. Prajurit-prajurit berpengalaman dimasukkan dalam pasukan di bawah komandonya. Al-Baraa' Ibn Malik dan Thabit Ibn Qays memimpin kontingen kaum Anshar, sementara Abu Hadhayfah dan Zayd memimpin kontingen kaum Muhajirin. Bertentangan dengan kebijakannya yang telah dinyatakan, Abu Bakr mengizinkan para veteran Badr untuk bergabung dengan pasukan Khalid. Di antara mereka yang bergabung dalam pasukan tersebut adalah 'Abdur Rahman Ibn Abi Bakr, 'Abdullah Ibn 'Umar, dan Abu Dujaanah sang pejuang terkenal dari Uhud, radhiyallahu 'anhum ajma'in.

Pertempuran 'Aqraba:

menemaninya ke medan perang. Khalid memerintahkannya untuk menjaganya.

Musaylamah menghadang gerak maju pasukan Muslim di dataran 'Aqraba. Di sinilah kedua pasukan yang berhadapan menyusun barisan mereka untuk pertempuran. Pertempuran yang berlangsung kemudian berlangsung sengit. Kekuatan pasukan Musaylamah berjumlah lebih dari 4.000 orang, sementara kekuatan pasukan Muslim tidak melebihi 13.000 orang. Selain kalah jumlah, pasukan Muslim juga menghadapi kelemahan tertentu. Terdapat perbedaan di antara suku-suku, antara kaum Anshar dan kaum Muhajirin.

Ketika pertempuran 'Aqraba berkobar, terdapat putra Musaylamah yang fasih berbicara, Sharhabeel, yang terus memprovokasi pasukan dan meningkatkan semangat mereka. Ia mengingatkan mereka akan kekejaman Khalid dan memperingatkan bahwa kecuali mereka bertempur dengan berani, wanita-wanita mereka akan dijadikan tawanan.

Badai debu menerpa lembah menghantam wajah-wajah kaum muslimin. Memanfaatkan kesempatan ini, pasukan Musaylamah meningkatkan tekanan mereka, dan kaum muslimin terpaksa mundur. Beberapa orang dari Banu Haneefah bahkan mencapai tenda Khalid tempat Majaa*ah dirantai dijaga oleh Layla. Orang-orang ini ingin membunuh Layla dan membebaskan Majaa*ah. Mujaa*ah menyerukan mereka agar tidak mengangkat tangan terhadap seorang wanita. Ia menginginkan mereka pergi membunuh beberapa lelaki. Orang-orang ini meninggalkan perkemahan dan berkata bahwa mereka akan kembali setelah beberapa waktu untuk membebaskan Majaa*ah. Dalam kekacauan yang terjadi, rombongan tersebut tidak dapat kembali, dan sementara itu kaum muslimin berhasil mengambil tindakan pencegahan.

Ketika pertempuran hari pertama berakhir, terdapat kegembiraan di perkemahan Musaylamah. Meskipun Khalid dipaksa mundur, ia menolak untuk mengakui kekalahan. Ia menyusun kembali pasukannya dalam komando kesukuan dan mendorong berbagai suku untuk

menunjukkan keberanian mereka di medan perang. Dari para penunggang kuda Makkah dan Madinah, ia membentuk pasukan cadangan yang terdiri dari seribu kavaleri dan menempatkan mereka di bawah komando pribadinya.
content_ar: Abu Qatadah, Al-Harith Ibn Rib*yy Al-Ansaaryy, testified that the prisoners offered الصلاة, بينما others said they neither gave Adhaan nor offered الصلاة. The prisoners retired for the night. At the dead of night, Khalid ordered that the night being cold, the prisoners should be kept warm, but this order was misunderstood to be an order for murder. وبناءً على ذلك, مالك and his male الصحابة were killed. It was Daraar Ibn Al-Azwad who murdered مالك [bn Nuwayrah. Having seen the مالك and his الصحابة killed, Khalid remarked, "إذا الله wills that something should occur, it surely occurs.” بعد the الموت of مالك, the entire tribe of Bani Tameem surrendered and professed الإيمان in الإسلام. Khalid immediately married Layla, the beautiful widow of مالك Ibn Nuwayrah. The campaign against the Bani Tameem was a masterstroke from the political point of view. It brought the entire tribe to the fold of الإسلام. From the military point of view the significance of the action at Bitaah was that the rear of Sajaah. the false Prophetess, was cut off, and she could no longer count on the support of Bani Tameem. The episode, غير أن, led to considerable scandal. In some quarters it was held that مالك was indeed a مسلم, and that he had been murdered merely لأن Khalid coveted his pretty الزوجة. Some of the Ansaar in the army of Khalid led by Abu Qatadah refused to fight under the command of Khalid. Abu Qatadah along with مالك's الأخ set out for Madeenah to lodge a complaint against Khalid قبل the Caliph. MuTameem was a distinguished poet, and he composed an elegy mourning the الموت of his الأخ, and condemning Khalid as his murderer. These الآيات became popular in Madeenah and those who listened to them grieved at the murder of مالك. Khalid was summoned to Madeenah and was asked to clarify his position. There were two charges against him: murdering a مسلم man and marrying his الزوجة. Khalid's defense of himself was that إذا وفقاً لـ النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, he was the "Sword of الله", such sword could not fall on the neck of a مسلم. On entering the Grand المسجد in his rusty armor with arrows stuck in his turban, Khalid was noticed by ~ عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab, who went up to him, pulled out the arrows and broke them into pieces. قال threateningly: "You have killed a مسلم in cold blood and taken his الزوجة for yours! By الله! You deserve to be stoned!" على الرغم من ذلك, عندما Khalid met the Caliph, he managed to defend himself and was excused, though he did not escape being criticized for his disgraceful marriage with Layla. “عمر was of the view that Khalid was to be blamed, and he should be suitably punished. أبو بكر felt that a military commander, Khalid was indispensable. His view was that even إذا it was held that Khalid was guilty of a lapse, such lapse could be passed over in the broader interest of الإسلام. Musaylamah in the Yamamah valley was posing a great threat
